description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular form and a woodgrain-patterned exterior casing. The front features a large, convex glass screen bordered by a thick, dark bezel, and a control panel on the right side with two prominent rotary dials and a speaker grille below. The control panel includes branding and a small colored indicator, suggesting analog tuning and channel selection. The sides and top are finished in a faux wood veneer, while the back is dominated by a protruding, black plastic housing typical of cathode ray tube (CRT) technology. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible ventilation slots on the rear and a sturdy, flat top surface. This television would be commonly found in urban homes, garages, or even discarded on sidewalks in city environments.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain exterior, large convex glass screen, and rotary dials on the front control panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large glass screen, two rotary dials, speaker grille, and branding on the right control panel.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the woodgrain finish and the depth of the CRT housing extending from the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the protruding black CRT housing and ventilation slots, with no controls or branding visible.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, emphasizing the woodgrain finish and the CRT's rear extension.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, dark brown and silver bezel with a woodgrain pattern, rectangular and robust.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of two rotary dials and a button on the front right panel
